question: What was the name of the towing vessel that capsized?
reference_answer: Baylor J. Tregre
supporting_source_spans: Title, Vessel Particulars section

question: What was the date of the casualty?
supporting_source_spans: Executive Summary, Casualty Summary table, Date field

question: What barge was being towed at the time of the incident?
reference_answer: MARMAC 27
supporting_source_spans: Executive Summary, Barge Description section

question: How many crewmembers were aboard the Baylor J. Tregre?
reference_answer: Four crewmembers (captain, mate, deckhand 1, deckhand 2)
supporting_source_spans: Crew and Watch Schedule section, Casualty Summary table

question: In what year was the Baylor J. Tregre originally built?
supporting_source_spans: Vessel Description section, Vessel Particulars table

question: At what time did the vessel depart from Houma?
reference_answer: About 0340 on May 11, 2024
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Initial Agreement and Departure subsection

question: When did the mate notice the squall forming?
reference_answer: Sometime after the phone call at approximately 1530, exact time not specified
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Observation of Forming Squall subsection

question: At what time did the mate make the distress call?
reference_answer: 1657 (5:57 PM central daylight time)
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Distress Call and Power Loss subsection

question: How many minutes elapsed between the vessel losing electrical power and the last AIS transmission?
reference_answer: Approximately 15 minutes (power loss at approximately 1657, last AIS transmission at 1712)
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence sections describing power loss and AIS transmission

question: At what time did the Coast Guard recover all four crew members?
reference_answer: 1808 (8:08 PM central daylight time)
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Sinking and Rescue subsection

question: Who ordered the mate to "turn the barge loose"?
reference_answer: The captain
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Tow Release Attempt subsection

question: Which crew member was the first to notice the vessel heeling 45° to port?
reference_answer: Deckhand 2 (who was below decks conducting clean-ups)
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Onset of Severe Weather subsection

question: What caused the vessel to heel to port according to crew testimony?
reference_answer: The force of wind on the MARMAC 27 (and particularly on the production platform), pushing the barge to the south, caused the towline to pull on the stern of the tugboat, causing it to heel to port
supporting_source_spans: Analysis section

question: What factor prevented the mate from releasing the tow when instructed to do so?
reference_answer: The doghouse was partially submerged and inaccessible; the winch engine was not running; and the engine room was inaccessible due to the extreme port heel
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Tow Release Attempt subsection, and Analysis section

question: Under what regulatory code was the Baylor J. Tregre required to demonstrate compliance?
reference_answer: Title 46 Code of Federal Regulations Subchapter M
supporting_source_spans: Background section, Vessel Description subsection

question: Was an emergency release function required by regulation for the tow winch in the wheelhouse?
reference_answer: No, emergency release function in the wheelhouse was not required by regulation
supporting_source_spans: Analysis section

question: How long was the tow wire connecting the Baylor J. Tregre to MARMAC 27?
reference_answer: About 800 feet long
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Tow Arrangement subsection

question: What was the estimated property damage to the Baylor J. Tregre?
reference_answer: $2 million estimated
supporting_source_spans: Executive Summary, Casualty Summary table, Property damage field

question: The crew reported different wind speed estimates than the nearest weather station; what were the respective ranges?
reference_answer: Crew estimated 85–100 mph (74–87 knots); closest NWS station (22–27 miles away) reported gusts of 48–62 knots
supporting_source_spans: Analysis section

question: What conflicting evidence exists regarding whether Special Marine Warnings reached the vessel?
reference_answer: The mate did not recall hearing updated weather forecasts over VHF radio, thus was unaware of Special Marine Warnings issued at 1501 and 1544, though whether they actually reached the vessel is not definitively established
supporting_source_spans: Analysis section

question: Was the phenomenon observed by the mate (wind gust, wind shear, possible waterspout) definitively identified in the investigation?
reference_answer: The exact nature of the storm phenomena (possible waterspout, wind shear vortex, or severe straight-line winds) was not definitively identified; the mate's description of 'maybe a waterspout' was not confirmed
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Analysis section

question: As of the final report date, what was the status of the investigation and conclusions?
reference_answer: Final Investigation Report with probable cause definitively determined (issued May 15, 2025)
supporting_source_spans: Report metadata, Probable Cause section

question: How far was the closest weather reporting station (KGLS) from the actual casualty site?
reference_answer: The closest NWS weather reporting station (KGLS) was located about 22 miles north of the casualty site; buoy stations were 25–27 miles away
supporting_source_spans: Additional Information section, Environmental Information, Reported Weather subsection

question: What impact did salvage operations have on the completeness of the physical evidence examination?
reference_answer: Salvage operations were conducted from May 18 to June 15; after the casualty, NTSB investigators found multiple fractures and indentations on the vessel superstructure that appeared to be from recovery activities.
supporting_source_spans: Additional Information section, Damage subsection

question: Why was the mate's contact with subsequent weather broadcasts over VHF radio uncertain?
reference_answer: The mate stated he did not recall hearing any updated weather forecasts over VHF radio after the initial phone call from shoreside, leaving uncertainty about what information actually reached the vessel
supporting_source_spans: Event Sequence section, Weather Information and Reports subsection
